title: IT Sr. Director
company: The Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine
raw_jd:
About the job

The Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine (SOM) is seeking an inaugural IT Sr. Director who will establish and lead the information technology function for the school. Reporting to the School of Medicine’s Vice Dean for Finance and Administration and the University Deputy Chief Information Officer, the IT Sr. Director advances and supports the school’s information technology and key data analytics infrastructure and services in order to advance the mission of the school. The role serves as the leader, domain expert, and strategic partner in the information technology, data infrastructure, and information security domains for the school. The IT Sr. Director provides counsel to senior leadership related to technology and business process direction. The incumbent represents Hopkins while working with external constituencies, including state, higher education, healthcare, and federal organizations, in the development of technology and/or regulatory policies and practices, as well as industry organizations that have an impact on the business of the institutions.

The position leads large-scale, complex projects requiring multi-disciplinary coordination and significant vendor collaboration. The incumbent works as a trusted partner for medicine, healthcare, and university communities and has as day-to-day responsibility for school IT operations. The IT Sr. Director will set strategy and provide technological leadership across the school, with a primary focus on administrative and educational technologies. This role requires a highly collaborative approach, working closely with university and health system IT organizations to ensure alignment, seamless integration, and valued solutions that meet the school’s diverse needs.

Specific Duties & Responsibilities

    Architect and implement a school-wide IT operating model and organization design, encompassing a central IT organization and coordinated departmental IT functions, to deliver a value-added IT and data strategy, enterprise architecture, and shared best practices.
    Establish and oversee information management and governance frameworks that provide agile, efficient oversight of the school’s IT ecosystem, including standards, policies, and lifecycle management.
    Lead the design, deployment, and continuous improvement of innovative educational technologies that enhance teaching, learning, assessment, and the student experience.
    Serve as the school’s advocate and subject-matter representative in university and health system IT committees, planning, and enterprise initiatives, ensuring school-specific use cases and requirements are addressed.
    Develop and execute a comprehensive information security strategy for the school, aligned with Hopkins policies and industry best practices; oversee risk management, compliance, and incident response in coordination with institutional security teams.
    Leads the federated organization of IT within the school of medicine, ensuring programs and individuals are well positioned to advance the needs of the school and divisions tri-partite mission: education, research and clinical.
    Manages key practices, committees, and processes to ensure school’s IT is well governed, runs efficiency, serves as a good partner, and provides value.
    As a member of the senior management team, lead strategic and tactical planning to achieve institutional goals by prioritizing initiatives and coordinating the evaluation, deployment, and management of current and future technologies.
    Develop technology infrastructure to anticipate the organization's needs, be cost-effective, reliable and compatible with existing and emerging technologies.
    Anticipate change and be agile to respond when technology requirements emerge and evolve.
    Based on understanding of institutional goals, mission, and culture, assess impact and effectiveness of technology to ensure it supports the institution’s needs.
    Provides guidance and is ultimately responsible for ongoing operations and capital budgets, a multi-year budget forecast and both strategic and operational planning.
    Balances available personnel and funding resources in support of meeting both operational and strategic imperatives. Recognizes situations that present themselves and takes action with executive leadership to address significant issues.
    Provides guidance for the development of technology related policies and procedures and represents IT on business-driven policy committees within and outside of Hopkins.
    Ensures that applicable Hopkins policies, practices, regulatory requirements are addressed and followed within his/her area of responsibility.
    Has overall responsibility for the design, development, and application of technical solutions that satisfy customer needs and are essential to the ongoing operations of the Johns Hopkins School of Medicine.
    Ensure continuous delivery of information technology support and services through oversight of service level agreements.
    Other duties as assigned.

Minimum Qualifications

    Bachelor’s degree.
    Ten years of progressively responsible IT management experience including five years of management/supervisory experience.
    Additional education may substitute for required experience and additional related experience may substitute for required education beyond a high school diploma/graduation equivalent, to the extent permitted by the JHU equivalency formula.

Preferred Qualifications

    Advanced degree in a technology or business field coupled and proven experience leading large scale, complex projects is desired.
    Experience with HIPAA and FERPA.

This salary range does not include all components of the School of Medicine's compensation program. This position may be eligible for a discretionary bonus. Therefore, the actual compensation paid to the selected candidate may vary slightly from the salary range stated herein. 

Classified Title: IT Sr. Director-SOM

Job Posting Title (Working Title): IT Sr. Director (School of Medicine - Information Technology)

Role/Level/Range: L/05/LJ

Starting Salary Range: $165,000 - $288,900 Annually ($230,000 targeted; Commensurate w/exp.)

Employee group: Full Time

Schedule: Mon-Fri, 8.30am-5.00pm

FLSA Status: Exempt

Location: Hybrid/School of Medicine Campus

Department name: SOM Admin HR General Administration

Personnel area: School of Medicine

Featured benefits

Medical insurance, Vision insurance, Dental insurance, 401(k), Paid maternity leave, Paid paternity leave, Commuter benefits, Student loan assistance, Tuition assistance, Disability insurance

score.skills_rationale: Good technical alignment with candidate's enterprise architecture, data strategy, and IT leadership background. However, the role emphasizes educational technology and academic IT which isn't prominent in candidate's experience. Strong match on enterprise architecture, data governance, and large-scale project leadership.
score.scope_rationale: Excellent scope match - this is an inaugural senior director role requiring building IT organization from ground up, which aligns perfectly with candidate's CFO-level reporting, enterprise architecture experience, and track record of leading large-scale initiatives like ERP implementations and organizational transformations.
score.pay_rationale: Salary range of $165K-$288K with $230K target falls within candidate's W2 target range of $160K-$220K, though at the higher end. Good alignment for a senior director role.
score.raw_analysis: Strong strategic leadership role at prestigious institution with excellent scope and pay alignment. The inaugural nature of the position plays to candidate's strengths in building organizations and enterprise architecture. While educational technology experience is limited, the enterprise IT leadership, data governance, and large-scale project experience are highly relevant. This represents a high-value opportunity for advancing into senior academic leadership.

cover_letters.content:
Dear Hiring Manager,

As the architect behind two major ERP transformations—the VA's $1.2B iFAMS implementation and Howard University's Workday deployment—I am uniquely positioned to establish and lead the inaugural IT function at Johns Hopkins School of Medicine. My 25+ years of enterprise IT leadership, combined with my proven track record of building IT organizations from the ground up and delivering complex, multi-stakeholder technology initiatives, directly aligns with your need for a strategic leader who can architect a school-wide IT operating model while managing day-to-day operations.

At the Department of Veterans Affairs, I currently lead data architecture and reporting for the Financial Management Business Transformation program, coordinating across finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders to deliver enterprise data pipelines and executive reporting systems. This mirrors the cross-functional collaboration required at Johns Hopkins, where I would serve as the bridge between medicine, healthcare, and university communities. My experience at Howard University is particularly relevant—during a period of executive disruption, I stepped in as one of three cross-functional drivers to stabilize a multi-million-dollar ERP implementation, establishing data governance frameworks and building enterprise BI architecture that supported critical decision-making.

My technical expertise spans the full spectrum of capabilities you're seeking: enterprise architecture, data strategy, information security, and large-scale project management. I've designed containerized environments and data platforms, established governance frameworks that improved audit readiness, and built systems serving tens of thousands of users. Beyond technical delivery, I've consistently operated at the senior leadership level—providing counsel on technology direction, representing organizations in external partnerships, and managing complex budgets and multi-year strategic planning.

The inaugural nature of this role is precisely what draws me to Johns Hopkins. Building IT organizations, establishing governance frameworks, and creating value-driven technology strategies is where I excel. I'm ready to leverage my proven experience in enterprise transformations, stakeholder management, and strategic IT leadership to establish a world-class IT function that advances the School of Medicine's mission.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

As Johns Hopkins School of Medicine embarks on establishing its inaugural IT organization, you face the complex challenge of building a comprehensive technology function from the ground up while ensuring seamless integration with existing university and health system infrastructure. My 25+ years of enterprise IT leadership, particularly in large-scale organizational transformations and ERP implementations, positions me to tackle this multifaceted challenge. Currently leading data architecture for the VA's $2.6B Financial Management Business Transformation, I've navigated the complexities of establishing new IT frameworks within established institutional environments—experience directly applicable to creating SOM's federated IT model.

Your need for someone who can architect enterprise-wide operating models resonates strongly with my recent work at Howard University, where I served as one of three cross-functional leaders driving a multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation during a period of executive disruption. When the CIO departed and CFO position was vacant, I stepped into a stabilizing role, helping establish data governance policies, implementing enterprise BI architecture, and coordinating across finance, HR, and IT stakeholders to deliver a successful deployment. This mirrors the collaborative, multi-disciplinary coordination required to build SOM's IT organization while working closely with university and health system partners.

The role's emphasis on strategic technology planning and budget oversight aligns perfectly with my track record of translating complex technical requirements into business value. At VitalSpring Technologies, I designed analytics dashboards that identified process bottlenecks and delivered over $70K in savings within six months. More recently, my work at the VA involves coordinating enterprise reporting systems that support financial operations and executive decision-making—the kind of strategic, value-driven approach essential for SOM's administrative and educational technology needs.

I'm particularly drawn to the opportunity to establish information security frameworks and governance processes in an academic medical environment. My CISSP certification, combined with hands-on experience designing audit-ready data architectures and establishing enterprise data validation processes, provides the foundation needed to develop comprehensive security strategies that balance academic openness with regulatory compliance. I welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience building resilient, integrated IT organizations can support Johns Hopkins School of Medicine's mission.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my strong interest in the inaugural IT Sr. Director position at Johns Hopkins School of Medicine. With 25+ years leading enterprise IT transformations and currently architecting data systems for the Department of Veterans Affairs' $2.6B ERP modernization, I bring the exact combination of strategic leadership and hands-on technical expertise needed to establish SOM's IT organization from the ground up. My experience driving multi-million-dollar ERP implementations at Howard University—where I served as one of three cross-functional leaders during executive transitions—demonstrates my ability to build resilient IT frameworks while navigating complex academic and regulatory environments.

Your inaugural role requires someone who can architect enterprise solutions while building organizational capability, which directly aligns with my track record of designing integrated data platforms, establishing governance frameworks, and leading cross-functional teams through large-scale system transformations. Having delivered enterprise architecture solutions across federal agencies and academic institutions, I understand the unique intersection of educational technology, regulatory compliance, and mission-critical operations that defines this role. I'm excited to bring my proven ability to translate institutional vision into scalable IT strategy to advance SOM's tri-partite mission.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

tailored_resume:
SALIL MANIKTAHLA
Springfield, VA  ·  571-215-8218  ·  salil.maniktahla@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/salilmaniktahla

SUMMARY
Strategic IT leader with 25+ years of experience architecting and implementing enterprise-wide technology and data infrastructure across complex institutional environments. Currently leading data architecture for the Department of Veterans Affairs' enterprise ERP modernization while bringing proven expertise in establishing IT operating models, governance frameworks, and organizational design. Track record of serving as trusted advisor to senior leadership, building IT organizations from the ground up, and leading large-scale, complex projects requiring multi-disciplinary coordination. Demonstrated success partnering with finance, academic, healthcare, and federal stakeholders to deliver innovative technology solutions that advance institutional missions.

CORE CAPABILITIES
Enterprise IT Strategy & Architecture
    • IT operating model design, organizational development, and governance frameworks
    • Enterprise architecture, technology infrastructure planning, and lifecycle management
    • Large-scale project leadership requiring multi-disciplinary coordination and vendor collaboration

Information Security & Compliance
    • Information security strategy development, risk management, and incident response
    • Regulatory compliance frameworks and enterprise policy development
    • Security architecture for sensitive data environments (financial, healthcare adjacency)

Educational & Administrative Technologies
    • Enterprise data platforms, reporting systems, and analytics infrastructure
    • ERP implementation and integration (Workday, iFAMS, legacy system modernization)
    • Technology solutions supporting institutional operations and decision-making

Leadership & Governance
    • Senior management team participation and strategic planning leadership
    • Cross-functional stakeholder management and external constituency representation
    • IT budget management, resource allocation, and multi-year forecasting

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

Senior Data & Analytics Architect (Contractor)
U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs – FMBT	Sep 2021 – Present
    • Lead enterprise architecture and technology strategy for large-scale ERP modernization initiative serving one of the nation's largest healthcare and administrative organizations
    • Architect and implement data infrastructure integrating legacy financial systems with modern ERP platforms, establishing governance frameworks and best practices across the enterprise
    • Serve as trusted technology advisor to senior leadership, providing strategic counsel on technology direction and business process optimization
    • Lead complex, multi-disciplinary projects requiring coordination across finance, acquisition, IT, and external vendor teams
    • Establish information management frameworks including data validation, reconciliation processes, and audit-ready architectures
    • Design and deploy innovative reporting and analytics solutions supporting executive decision-making and institutional operations
    • Coordinate with federal stakeholders and external constituencies on technology policies and regulatory compliance

Business Intelligence Analyst – Finance
Howard University	Apr 2020 – Jul 2024
    • Played key technology leadership role in multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation as one of three cross-functional drivers during period of executive disruption, helping stabilize delivery and drive program to successful deployment
    • Led enterprise Data Governance initiative, architecting policies, implementation strategy, and organizational frameworks that improved data quality and institutional oversight
    • Served as subject-matter representative for analytics and reporting, collaborating directly with Deputy CFO and senior leadership team
    • Designed and implemented enterprise BI data architecture and data warehouse supporting institutional strategic and operational planning
    • Established KPI frameworks, reporting structures, and dashboard systems enhancing administrative efficiency and decision-making capabilities
    • Developed comprehensive data management policies and validation processes aligned with institutional governance requirements

BI Partner / Strategist (Independent Consultant)
Multiple Clients	Jul 2016 – Mar 2020
    • Led technology strategy and implementation across diverse institutional environments, delivering enterprise data platforms and governance solutions
    • Architected BI product deployments aligned with organizational governance and change management processes
    • Provided technology leadership and mentoring across engineering teams, supporting hiring, onboarding, and professional development initiatives
    • Built comprehensive analytics infrastructure supporting 350+ users globally with 40+ integrated dashboard solutions
    • Evaluated and optimized technology systems for performance, scalability, security, and long-term maintainability

VitalSpring Technologies
BI Senior Manager	May 2008 – Jul 2009
    • Served as Product Manager for enterprise BI/analytics initiatives, establishing technology strategy and organizational frameworks
    • Led Agile delivery methodologies across technology teams, mentoring staff and coordinating multi-disciplinary project execution
    • Designed innovative technology solutions identifying operational bottlenecks and process improvements, achieving $70K+ cost savings in under 6 months
    • Architected healthcare analytics platforms supporting complex regulatory and operational requirements

EARLIER EXPERIENCE

Senior BI Consultant – Claraview (Teradata), 2006–2008
    • Technical architect and strategic leader for 25,000-user enterprise reporting platform serving Fortune 100 client (Lowe's)
    • Defined enterprise KPIs, analytics frameworks, and governance standards for large-scale institutional technology deployment

BI Consultant – Kinetic Networks, 2005–2006
    • Delivered enterprise analytics solutions for major technology and financial institutions including Google, Yahoo, and Wells Fargo

IT Specialist – U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, 2002–2005
    • Led team of 6 developers and engineers while defining comprehensive IT strategy for National Center for Environmental Economics
    • Established technology policies, procedures, and governance frameworks for federal research and administrative operations

Operations Manager – MicroStrategy, 1999–2001
    • Led engineering team delivering integrated enterprise analytics systems and technology infrastructure
    • Achieved significant cost optimization (~$312K annually) through strategic vendor management and technology planning

SELECTED SYSTEMS & INNOVATION
    • Designed secure knowledge management platform using local LLM/RAG architecture with role-based access controls and comprehensive audit logging
    • Architected containerized enterprise environments supporting distributed data and application services
    • Built integrated technology systems combining data pipelines, event-driven automation, and comprehensive monitoring infrastructure
    • Operate advanced technology infrastructure (10GbE backbone, 100TB+ storage, Docker-based services, enterprise-grade networking)

TECHNICAL SKILLS
Data & Analytics: SQL, Python, ETL/ELT, Data Modeling, Enterprise Data Pipelines
BI Platforms: Tableau, Power BI, MicroStrategy
Infrastructure: Linux (Ubuntu), Docker, Networking, Distributed Systems, Enterprise Architecture
Cloud: Azure (data services), CI/CD practices, Enterprise Integration
Security: Information security frameworks, compliance management, risk assessment
AI/LLM: RAG pipelines, embeddings, vector search, local model deployment

CERTIFICATIONS & EDUCATION
B.A., Washington University in St. Louis
CISSP  ·  Certified Scrum Master  ·  Tableau Certified Professional  ·  MicroStrategy Certified Developer

emails.subject: Confirmation from The Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine
emails.body:
Johns Hopkins University No Reply <system@successfactors.com>
	
4:22 PM (0 minutes ago)
	
	
to me

 

Greetings,

Thank you for submitting an application for the IT Sr. Director (School of Medicine - Information Technology) position. Your application has been received by the Talent Acquisition Team and is pending review. You may be contacted by a Johns Hopkins University representative to schedule an interview if your experience and qualifications are deemed a suitable match for the position.

We appreciate your interest and time in completing your application. For information on your status, you may check your profile page at any time for updates. To update your profile or see more openings at Johns Hopkins University, visit our Careers site at hiring.jhu.edu.

Thank you,
The Johns Hopkins University Talent Acquisition Team
